skill.name: lark-minutes
skill.description: Retrieve Lark Minutes metadata such as title, cover, duration, owner ID, and meeting URL from a valid minutes token.
skill.summary: Fetch basic Lark Minutes metadata and guide users to related notes resources.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs agents to run authenticated lark-cli commands. The network risk is also real because those commands query Lark services, while the filesystem, entropy, weak cryptography, and hardcoded URL findings are false positives from Markdown links, Chinese text, and example URLs.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated External CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to run lark-cli commands for schema lookup and minutes retrieval. This is expected for the skill, but it executes local commands and may access authenticated Lark tenant data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and semantically required by the skill. The commands are constrained to lark-cli, so the risk is operational rather than evidence of malware.

medium_findings.title: Meeting Metadata and Notes Link Exposure
medium_findings.description: The skill says to default to querying basic metadata and related notes product information when the user is unclear. This can reveal meeting titles, owners, durations, URLs, or related artifact links from authenticated accounts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions directly describe default retrieval behavior for meeting metadata and related products. The file also limits direct reading of note content, which reduces severity.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings point to Markdown references to sibling skill documentation. They are documentation links, not file reads or writes executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain Markdown links only. No evidence found of runtime filesystem access, file writes, or user-controlled path use.

low_findings.title: Example URLs Flagged as Network Indicators
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are examples showing how to extract a minutes token from a Lark or Feishu URL. They do not define an exfiltration endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are clearly presented as examples for token extraction. The real network behavior comes from lark-cli API calls, not from these literal examples.

low_findings.title: Entropy and Weak Cryptography Heuristics Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged the description and file entropy, but the file is readable Markdown with Chinese text and command examples. No encoded payload or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found plain YAML front matter and natural language instructions. No evidence found of encrypted content, decoding logic, or weak cryptographic algorithms.

dangerous_patterns.title: lark-cli Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses lark-cli commands to inspect schemas and query Lark Minutes data. Agents should run these commands only for trusted user requests and authenticated workspaces.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are visible in fenced bash blocks and are part of normal operation. They remain sensitive because they can access private meeting metadata.

content.value_statement: Meeting links often hide the token needed for Lark Minutes API calls. This skill extracts the token and retrieves trusted metadata through lark-cli.
actual_capabilities: Extracts a minute_token from a Lark or Feishu minutes URL.
Shows the lark-cli schema command before native API use.
Retrieves basic minutes metadata with lark-cli minutes get.
Identifies returned fields such as title, cover, duration, owner ID, and URL.
Points users to lark-cli vc notes for transcripts, summaries, tasks, and chapters.
limitations: Requires lark-cli to be installed and authenticated.
Retrieves basic metadata, not full transcript or summary content.
Needs a valid minutes token or supported minutes URL.

use_cases.title: Verify meeting metadata
use_cases.description: Check the title, owner, duration, cover, and URL for a shared Lark Minutes link.

use_cases.title: Prepare meeting follow-up
use_cases.description: Find the related notes artifacts before sharing summaries or action item links with a team.

use_cases.title: Support API troubleshooting
use_cases.description: Confirm the correct minute_token and inspect the required lark-cli schema before API calls.

prompt_templates.title: Get basic metadata
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with one minutes URL.
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the basic metadata for this Lark Minutes URL: <paste URL>.

prompt_templates.title: Extract token first
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the minute_token from this Feishu Minutes URL, then retrieve its title, owner, duration, and URL.

prompt_templates.title: Check related notes
prompt_templates.prompt: For this minute_token, get the basic metadata and show any related notes artifact links without reading full transcript content.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect schema and query
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the schema check for Lark Minutes get, then query this minute_token and summarize the returned metadata fields.

output_examples.input: Retrieve metadata for a Lark Minutes link.
output_examples.output: Title: Weekly Planning. Duration: 42 minutes. Owner ID: listed by Lark. URL: the canonical minutes link.

output_examples.input: Extract the token from a Feishu Minutes URL.
output_examples.output: The minute_token is the final path segment before any query parameters. Use that token for the metadata lookup.

output_examples.input: Show related meeting note products without reading them.
output_examples.output: The response includes links to available notes artifacts. Full transcript, summary, tasks, and chapters are not opened unless requested.

best_practices: Confirm the user has permission to access the meeting before running lark-cli.
Run the schema command before native API calls to verify parameter format.
Share links to notes artifacts before reading their full content.
anti_patterns: Do not guess minute_token values from incomplete links.
Do not read transcripts or summaries when the user only asked for metadata.
Do not run lark-cli against untrusted tenant data without user confirmation.

faq.question: What does this skill retrieve?
faq.answer: It retrieves basic Lark Minutes metadata such as title, cover, duration, owner ID, and URL.

faq.question: Can it read full transcripts?
faq.answer: Not directly. It points users to related lark-cli notes commands for transcripts, summaries, tasks, and chapters.

faq.question: What input does it need?
faq.answer: It needs a valid minute_token or a Lark or Feishu Minutes URL that contains the token.

faq.question: Does it require authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. lark-cli must be installed, authenticated, and authorized with the required read scope.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private meetings?
faq.answer: Use it only when the user has permission to access the meeting and share its metadata.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
